title: JS Variables
description: Learn to declare variables with let and const, work with strings and numbers, and use template literals to build dynamic text.

lessons.title: Constants
lessons.description: Use <kbd>const</kbd> to declare a variable that cannot be reassigned. Constants are great for values that stay the same throughout your program.
lessons.task: Declare a constant named <kbd>name</kbd> with the value <kbd>"Ada"</kbd>

lessons.title: Let Variables
lessons.description: Use <kbd>let</kbd> to declare a variable that can be reassigned later. This is useful when you need to update a value.
lessons.task: Declare a variable <kbd>score</kbd> with <kbd>let</kbd> and set it to <kbd>0</kbd>

lessons.title: Template Literals
lessons.description: Template literals use backticks <kbd>`</kbd> and <kbd>${}</kbd> to embed expressions inside strings. They make building dynamic text much easier than string concatenation.
lessons.task: Create a <kbd>const msg</kbd> using a template literal: <kbd>`Hi, ${name}!`</kbd>

validations.message: Use <kbd>${}</kbd> to embed the variable inside the template
Embed <kbd>name</kbd> inside a template literal with backticks
Assign the result to a constant named <kbd>msg</kbd>
